### Post by rfkrocktk on 2009-12-30
I'm currently unable to get rid of an annoying "Input Not Supported" box on my left monitor. I'm using an Nvidia card, and in 9.04, I was able to get around this problem by using 
```
Option "UseEDIDFreqs" "FALSE"
``` in my xorg.conf file. Did X change that option or something? 

Here is my xorg.conf file for further reference: ```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@crested)  Sun Feb  1 20:25:37 UTC 2009

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder58)  Fri Aug 14 18:33:37 PDT 2009

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "LCD R912E"
    HorizSync       31.0 - 95.0
    VertRefresh     55.0 - 85.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor1"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "LCD R912E"
    HorizSync       31.0 - 95.0
    VertRefresh     55.0 - 85.0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7600 GT"
    Option         "UseEDIDFreqs" "FALSE"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7600 GT"
    Option         "UseEDIDFreqs" "FALSE"
    BusID          "PCI:6:0:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "TwinView" "1"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "DFP-0: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP-1: nvidia-auto-select +1280+0"
# Removed Option "TwinView" "0"
# Removed Option "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "DFP-0"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1280x1024 +0+0"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1280x1024 +1280+0, DFP-1: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "DFP-1"
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1280x1024 +0+0, DFP-1: nvidia-auto-select +1280+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Device1"
    Monitor        "Monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP-1: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

Can anyone help me out with this?

### Post by rfkrocktk on 2009-12-31
I was able to get it SOMEWHAT working with the following xorg.conf file: 

```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@crested)  Sun Feb  1 20:25:37 UTC 2009


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "LCD R912E"
    HorizSync       60.0 - 60.0
    VertRefresh     60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"

    #Option	"UseEDID" "FALSE"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7600 GT"
    Option         "UseEDIDFreqs" "FALSE"
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1280x1024 +0+0, DFP-1: nvidia-auto-select +1280+0"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "DFP-0"
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1280x1024 +0+0, DFP-1: 1280x1024 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

However, now, I can only get my max resolution up to 1280x960 according to nvidia-settings. Is there something I've done wrong?  Any help would be most appreciated, I'm a beginner when it comes to dealing with X.

### Post by dcstar on 2009-12-31
> **rfkrocktk said:**
> I'm currently unable to get rid of an annoying "Input Not Supported" box on my left monitor.
...........
Can anyone help me out with this?

That is caused by an incorrect Resolution/Refresh combination being set for that monitor (or the auto-detection is not working correctly).

I thought that the default xorg.conf using auto detect would take care of this, here is one from my 9.04 system you may want to try:

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# Note that some configuration settings that could be done previously
# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
# here are ignored.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by rfkrocktk on 2009-12-31
I'm using an NVIDIA card, so I think that's why everything's all weird. I'm trying to use dual display, eg 2 monitors side by side, so I thought I had to use nvidia-settings to do it. If there's a simpler way to configure things without all of the extra NVIDIA stuff, I'm in. I'll give your conf file a try and see if it helps any.

---

### Post by rfkrocktk on 2010-01-13
SOLVED!!!

I had to read the documentation on the NVIDIA Driver and roll my own xorg.conf file, but here it is:

```
# Generated by HARD WORK via TK
Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7600 GT"
    Option         "UseEDIDFreqs" "FALSE"
    Option "TwinView"
    Option "MetaModes" "DFP-0: 1280x1024 +0+0, DFP-1: 1280x1024 +1280+0"
	Option "HorizSync" "DFP-0: 64; DFP-1: 60-70"
	Option "VertRefresh" "DFP-0: 60-85; DFP-1: 60-85"
EndSection

```

Again, for posterity's sake, I'm using an NVIDIA Geforce 7600 GT video card with two Rosewill R912E monitors. If you can get your hands on one of these, DON'T. I'm definitely getting non-Rosewill monitors next time around.

---
